AgentModel.description:
Configuration model for an agent in the DAO AI framework.

Agents combine an LLM with tools and middleware to create systems that can
reason about tasks, decide which tools to use, and iteratively work towards solutions.

Middleware replaces the previous pre_agent_hook and post_agent_hook patterns,
providing a more flexible and composable way to customize agent behavior.

name.description: Unique agent name used for identification in multi-agent orchestration.
name.title: Name

description.description: Human-readable description shown when the LLM selects handoff targets.
description.title: Description

model.description: LLM model configuration (serving endpoint name, temperature, etc.).

tools.description: Tools available to this agent during reasoning.
tools.title: Tools

guardrails.description: Guardrails that evaluate this agent's inputs and/or outputs.
guardrails.title: Guardrails

prompt.description: System prompt as an inline string or a PromptModel referencing the MLflow Prompt Registry.
prompt.title: Prompt

handoff_prompt.description: Additional instructions appended to the prompt during multi-agent handoff.
handoff_prompt.title: Handoff Prompt

middleware.description: List of middleware to apply to this agent.
middleware.title: Middleware

response_format.description: Structured output format (Pydantic type, JSON schema, or ResponseFormatModel).
response_format.title: Response Format

AgentModel.title: AgentModel

AppModel.description: Application-level configuration for deployment, model registration, and orchestration.

name.description: Unique application name used for the serving endpoint and model registration.
name.title: Name

service_principal.description: Service principal credentials injected as environment variables during Model Serving deployment.

log_level.description: Logging verbosity level (TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR).

registered_model.description: Unity Catalog registered model where the agent is logged.

endpoint_name.description: Model Serving endpoint name. Defaults to the app name if not specified.
endpoint_name.title: Endpoint Name

tags.description: Key-value tags attached to the registered model version.
tags.title: Tags

scale_to_zero.description: Whether the serving endpoint scales to zero when idle.
scale_to_zero.title: Scale To Zero

environment_vars.description: Environment variables set on the serving endpoint or Databricks App.
environment_vars.title: Environment Vars

budget_policy_id.description: Databricks budget policy ID for cost attribution.
budget_policy_id.title: Budget Policy Id

workload_size.description: Model Serving workload size (Small, Medium, Large).

permissions.description: Access control list for the serving endpoint.
permissions.title: Permissions

agents.description: List of agent definitions. At least one is required.
agents.title: Agents

orchestration.description: Multi-agent orchestration mode (supervisor or swarm). Auto-configured if omitted.

alias.description: Model version alias (e.g., 'champion') assigned after registration.
alias.title: Alias

initialization_hooks.description: Functions called once at startup after config is loaded.
initialization_hooks.title: Initialization Hooks

input_example.description: Example chat payload logged alongside the model for documentation and testing.

chat_history.description: Chat history summarization settings to manage long conversations.

code_paths.description: Additional Python file paths bundled with the model artifact.
code_paths.title: Code Paths

pip_requirements.description: Extra pip packages installed in the serving environment.
pip_requirements.title: Pip Requirements

python_version.description: Python version for Model Serving deployment. Defaults to 3.12 which is supported by Databricks Model Serving. This allows deploying from environments with different Python versions (e.g., Databricks Apps with 3.11).
python_version.title: Python Version

deployment_target.description: Default deployment target. If not specified, defaults to MODEL_SERVING. Can be overridden via CLI --target flag. Options: 'model_serving' or 'apps'.

trace_location.description: Unity Catalog location for storing MLflow traces in OTEL-format Delta tables. Accepts a schema reference or 'catalog.schema' string. When set, set_experiment_trace_location() is called at startup for both Model Serving and Databricks Apps deployments.

monitoring.description: Production monitoring configuration. When present, scorers are registered to continuously evaluate production traces. Works with both experiment-based traces and UC OTEL trace tables. When trace_location is also configured, the SQL warehouse from trace_location is used for monitoring.

AppModel.title: AppModel

ChatHistoryModel.description:
Configuration for chat history summarization.

Attributes:
    model: The LLM to use for generating summaries.
    max_tokens: Maximum tokens to keep after summarization (the "keep" threshold).
        After summarization, recent messages totaling up to this many tokens are preserved.
    max_tokens_before_summary: Token threshold that triggers summarization.
        When conversation exceeds this, summarization runs. Mutually exclusive with
        max_messages_before_summary. If neither is set, defaults to max_tokens * 10.
    max_messages_before_summary: Message count threshold that triggers summarization.
        When conversation exceeds this many messages, summarization runs.
        Mutually exclusive with max_tokens_before_summary.

model.description: LLM used to generate conversation summaries.

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens to keep after summarization.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

max_messages_before_summary.description: Message count threshold that triggers summarization
max_messages_before_summary.title: Max Messages Before Summary

ChatHistoryModel.title: ChatHistoryModel

input.description: Chat messages (alias for 'messages'). Provide either input or messages.

messages.title: Messages

custom_inputs.description: Extra inputs forwarded to the agent (e.g., configurable with thread_id).
custom_inputs.title: Custom Inputs

ColumnInfo.description:
Column metadata for dynamic schema generation in structured output.

When provided, column information is embedded directly into the JSON schema
that with_structured_output sends to the LLM, improving filter accuracy.

The optional ``description`` field lets users annotate a column with semantic
context (e.g. example values, business meaning).  Descriptions are embedded
into JSON schemas and prompt context that pipeline components (decomposition,
routing, verification, reranking) generate from the column metadata.

name.description: Column name as it appears in the database
name.title: Name

type.description: Column data type for value validation
type.title: Type

operators.description: Valid filter operators for this column
operators.title: Operators

description.description: Human-readable description of the column for LLM context. Include example values or business meaning to improve filter accuracy (e.g. 'Brand/manufacturer (MILWAUKEE, DEWALT, etc.)').
description.title: Description

ColumnInfo.title: ColumnInfo

default_value.title: Default Value

options.description: Ordered list of variable sources tried until one returns a non-None value.
options.title: Options

CompositeVariableModel.title: CompositeVariableModel

ContextAwareCacheEvalDatasetModel.description:
Dataset for context-aware cache threshold optimization.

Contains pairs of questions/contexts to evaluate whether thresholds
correctly identify semantic matches.

Example:
    dataset:
      name: my_cache_eval_dataset
      description: "Evaluation data for cache tuning"
      entries:
        - question: "What are total sales?"
          # ... entry fields

name.description: Unique name for this evaluation dataset.
name.title: Name

description.description: Human-readable description of the evaluation dataset.
description.title: Description

entries.description: List of question/context pair entries for evaluation.
entries.title: Entries

ContextAwareCacheEvalDatasetModel.title: ContextAwareCacheEvalDatasetModel

ContextAwareCacheEvalEntryModel.description:
Single evaluation entry for context-aware cache threshold optimization.

Represents a pair of question/context combinations to evaluate
whether the cache should return a hit or miss.

Example:
    entry:
      question: "What are total sales?"
      question_embedding: [0.1, 0.2, ...]  # Pre-computed
      context: "Previous: Show me revenue"
      context_embedding: [0.1, 0.2, ...]
      cached_question: "Show total sales"
      cached_question_embedding: [0.1, 0.2, ...]
      cached_context: "Previous: Show me revenue"
      cached_context_embedding: [0.1, 0.2, ...]
      expected_match: true

question.description: Incoming user question to evaluate against the cache.
question.title: Question

question_embedding.description: Pre-computed embedding vector for the question.
question_embedding.title: Question Embedding

context_embedding.description: Pre-computed embedding vector for the context.
context_embedding.title: Context Embedding

cached_question.description: Previously cached question to compare against.
cached_question.title: Cached Question

cached_question_embedding.description: Pre-computed embedding vector for the cached question.
cached_question_embedding.title: Cached Question Embedding

cached_context.description: Context that was stored with the cached question.
cached_context.title: Cached Context

cached_context_embedding.description: Pre-computed embedding vector for the cached context.
cached_context_embedding.title: Cached Context Embedding

expected_match.description: Whether the pair should be a cache hit (true) or miss (false). None = use LLM judge.
expected_match.title: Expected Match

ContextAwareCacheEvalEntryModel.title: ContextAwareCacheEvalEntryModel

ContextAwareCacheOptimizationModel.description:
Configuration for context-aware cache threshold optimization.

Uses Optuna Bayesian optimization to find optimal threshold values
that maximize cache hit accuracy (F1 score by default).

Example:
    optimizations:
      cache_threshold_optimizations:
        my_optimization:
          name: optimize_cache_thresholds
          cache_parameters: *my_cache_params
          dataset: *my_eval_dataset
          judge_model: databricks-meta-llama-3-3-70b-instruct
          n_trials: 50
          metric: f1

name.description: Unique name for this optimization run (used as the Optuna study name).
name.title: Name

cache_parameters.description: Cache configuration whose thresholds serve as the starting point for optimization.

dataset.description: Evaluation dataset with question/context pairs and expected match labels.

judge_model.description: LLM judge for evaluating match quality when expected_match is None.
judge_model.title: Judge Model

n_trials.description: Number of Bayesian optimization trials to run.
n_trials.title: N Trials

metric.description: Optimization metric to maximize (f1, precision, recall, or fbeta).
metric.title: Metric

beta.description: Beta parameter for the fbeta metric (higher = favor recall over precision).
beta.title: Beta

seed.description: Random seed for reproducible optimization results.
seed.title: Seed

ContextAwareCacheOptimizationModel.title: ContextAwareCacheOptimizationModel

DatabaseModel.description:
Configuration for database connections supporting both Databricks Lakebase and standard PostgreSQL.

Authentication is inherited from IsDatabricksResource. Additionally supports:
- user/password: For user-based database authentication

Connection Types (determined by fields provided):
- Databricks Lakebase: Provide `instance_name` (authentication optional, supports ambient auth)
- Standard PostgreSQL: Provide `host` (authentication required via user/password)

Note: For Lakebase connections, `name` is optional and defaults to `instance_name`.
For PostgreSQL connections, `name` is required.

Example Databricks Lakebase (minimal):
```yaml
databases:
  my_lakebase:
    instance_name: my-lakebase-instance  # name defaults to instance_name
```

Example Databricks Lakebase with Service Principal:
```yaml
databases:
  my_lakebase:
    instance_name: my-lakebase-instance
    service_principal:
      client_id:
        env: SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_CLIENT_ID
      client_secret:
        scope: my-scope
        secret: sp-client-secret
    workspace_host:
      env: DATABRICKS_HOST
```

Example Databricks Lakebase with Ambient Authentication:
```yaml
databases:
  my_lakebase:
    instance_name: my-lakebase-instance
    on_behalf_of_user: true
```

Example Standard PostgreSQL:
```yaml
databases:
  my_postgres:
    name: my-database
    host: my-postgres-host.example.com
    port: 5432
    database: my_db
    user: my_user
    password:
      env: PGPASSWORD
```

instance_name.title: Instance Name

node_count.title: Node Count

user.title: User

DatabricksAppModel.description:
Configuration for a Databricks App resource.

The `name` is the unique instance name of the Databricks App within the workspace.
The `url` is dynamically retrieved from the workspace client by calling
`apps.get(name)` and returning the app's URL.

Example:
    ```yaml
    resources:
      apps:
        my_app:
          name: my-databricks-app
    ```

DecompositionModel.description:
Query decomposition settings for instructed retrieval.

Decomposes complex user queries into multiple focused subqueries with
metadata filters, executed in parallel and merged using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF).

Example:
    ```yaml
    instructed:
      decomposition:
        model: *fast_llm
        max_subqueries: 3
        rrf_k: 60
        normalize_filter_case: uppercase
        examples:
          - query: "cheap drills"
            filters: {"price <": 100}
    ```

model.description: LLM for query decomposition (smaller/faster model recommended)

rrf_k.description: RRF constant (lower values weight top ranks more heavily)
rrf_k.title: Rrf K

examples.description: Few-shot examples for domain-specific filter translation
examples.title: Examples

normalize_filter_case.description: Auto-normalize filter string values to uppercase or lowercase
normalize_filter_case.title: Normalize Filter Case

DecompositionModel.title: DecompositionModel

EvaluationModel.description:
Configuration for MLflow GenAI offline evaluation.

Attributes:
    model: LLM model used as the judge for LLM-based scorers (e.g., Guidelines, Safety).
           This model evaluates agent responses during evaluation.
    table: Table to store evaluation results.
    num_evals: Number of evaluation samples to generate.
    replace: If True, drop and recreate the evaluation table and dataset.
        If False, reuse existing resources. Defaults to False.
    agent_description: Description of the agent for evaluation data generation.
    question_guidelines: Guidelines for generating evaluation questions.
    custom_inputs: Custom inputs to pass to the agent during evaluation.
    guidelines: List of guideline configurations for Guidelines scorers.

model.description: LLM model used as the judge for LLM-based evaluation scorers.

table.description: Unity Catalog table where evaluation results are stored.

num_evals.description: Number of evaluation samples to generate from the agent.
num_evals.title: Num Evals

replace.description: If True, drop and recreate the evaluation table and dataset. If False, reuse existing resources.
replace.title: Replace

agent_description.title: Agent Description

question_guidelines.description: Guidelines for the synthetic question generator (e.g., topic focus, difficulty).
question_guidelines.title: Question Guidelines

custom_inputs.description: Extra key-value inputs forwarded to the agent during evaluation runs.
custom_inputs.title: Custom Inputs

guidelines.description: Guideline configurations for Guidelines scorers used during evaluation.
guidelines.title: Guidelines

GenieContextAwareCacheParametersModel.description:
Configuration for PostgreSQL-backed context-aware cache.

Extends the base context-aware cache configuration with database-specific
fields for table management and prompt history tracking.

time_to_live_seconds.description: Cache entry TTL in seconds. None or negative = entries never expire. Default: 1 day.
time_to_live_seconds.title: Time To Live Seconds

similarity_threshold.description: Minimum similarity score (0-1) for question matching.
similarity_threshold.title: Similarity Threshold

context_similarity_threshold.description: Minimum similarity score (0-1) for conversation context matching.
context_similarity_threshold.title: Context Similarity Threshold

question_weight.description: Weight for question similarity in the combined score (0-1). Computed as 1 - context_weight if omitted.
question_weight.title: Question Weight

context_weight.description: Weight for context similarity in the combined score (0-1). Computed as 1 - question_weight if omitted.
context_weight.title: Context Weight

embedding_model.description: Embedding model endpoint for generating similarity vectors.
embedding_model.title: Embedding Model

embedding_dims.description: Embedding vector dimensions. Auto-detected from the model if not set.
embedding_dims.title: Embedding Dims

warehouse.description: SQL warehouse used by the Genie API for query execution.

context_window_size.description: Number of previous conversation turns included as context for matching.
context_window_size.title: Context Window Size

max_context_tokens.description: Maximum token length for context to prevent oversized embeddings.
max_context_tokens.title: Max Context Tokens

database.description: PostgreSQL or Lakebase database for persistent cache storage.

table_name.description: Table name for storing cache entries in the database.
table_name.title: Table Name

prompt_history_table.description: Table name for storing prompt history used in context-aware matching.
prompt_history_table.title: Prompt History Table

max_prompt_history_length.description: Maximum number of prompts to keep per conversation.
max_prompt_history_length.title: Max Prompt History Length

use_genie_api_for_history.description: Fall back to the Genie API when local prompt history is empty.
use_genie_api_for_history.title: Use Genie Api For History

prompt_history_ttl_seconds.description: TTL for prompt history entries in seconds. None = use the cache TTL.
prompt_history_ttl_seconds.title: Prompt History Ttl Seconds

ivfflat_lists.description: Number of IVFFlat index lists for pg_vector. None = auto-computed as max(100, sqrt(row_count)).
ivfflat_lists.title: Ivfflat Lists

ivfflat_probes.description: Number of IVFFlat lists to probe per query. None = auto-computed as max(10, sqrt(lists)).
ivfflat_probes.title: Ivfflat Probes

ivfflat_candidates.description: Number of top-K candidates retrieved before Python-side reranking.
ivfflat_candidates.title: Ivfflat Candidates

GuardrailModel.description:
Configuration for a guardrail.

Guardrails evaluate agent responses against quality or safety criteria.
Two configuration modes are supported:

1. **Custom (LLM-judge)** -- provide *model* and *prompt*.  A
   ``JudgeScorer`` is created using ``mlflow.genai.judges.make_judge``.
2. **Scorer-based** -- provide *scorer* (and optionally *scorer_args*).
   Any ``mlflow.genai.scorers.base.Scorer`` class can be used,
   including built-in ``GuardrailsScorer`` validators such as
   ``ToxicLanguage`` and ``DetectPII``.

The two modes are mutually exclusive.

Attributes:
    name: Name identifying this guardrail.
    model: LLM model for the judge.  Accepts a string (model name) or
        ``LLMModel``.  Required when using the custom judge mode.
    prompt: Evaluation instructions using ``{{ inputs }}`` and
        ``{{ outputs }}`` template variables.  Required when using
        the custom judge mode.
    scorer: Fully qualified name of an MLflow ``Scorer`` class
        (e.g. ``"mlflow.genai.scorers.guardrails.DetectPII"``).
        Required when using the scorer-based mode.
    scorer_args: Keyword arguments forwarded to the scorer constructor
        (e.g. ``{"pii_entities": ["CREDIT_CARD", "SSN"]}``).
    hub: Optional guardrails-ai hub URI for the scorer validator
        (e.g. ``"hub://guardrails/toxic_language"``).  When set,
        the validator is auto-installed at startup if the
        ``GUARDRAILSAI_API_KEY`` environment variable is present.
        Only valid when *scorer* is also set.
    num_retries: Maximum retry attempts when evaluation fails (default: 3).
    fail_on_error: If True, block responses when the evaluation call
        itself errors (e.g. scorer exception, network timeout).
        If False (default), let responses through on evaluation
        errors.
    max_context_length: Max character length for extracted tool context
        (default: 8000).
    apply_to: When to run this guardrail.  ``"input"`` runs before
        the model (on user messages), ``"output"`` runs after the
        model (on agent responses), ``"both"`` runs in both places
        (default: ``"both"``).

name.description: Name identifying this guardrail.
name.title: Name

model.description: LLM model for the judge. Required for custom judge mode.
model.title: Model

prompt.description: Evaluation instructions using {{ inputs }} and {{ outputs }} template variables. Required for custom judge mode.
prompt.title: Prompt

scorer.description: Fully qualified name of an MLflow Scorer class (e.g., 'mlflow.genai.scorers.guardrails.DetectPII'). Required for scorer-based mode.
scorer.title: Scorer

scorer_args.title: Scorer Args

num_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts when the evaluation call fails.

fail_on_error.description: If true, block responses when the evaluation itself errors. If false, let responses through on errors.

HandoffRouteModel.description:
Configuration model for a handoff route in a swarm.

A handoff route specifies a target agent and whether the handoff should be
deterministic (always route to this agent) or agentic (LLM decides via tool call).

When ``is_deterministic`` is ``True``, the source agent will **always** transfer
control to this target agent after completing its turn, without requiring the
LLM to invoke a handoff tool. This is useful for pipeline-style workflows
where the routing order is predetermined.

When ``is_deterministic`` is ``False`` (the default), a handoff tool is created
for the target agent and the LLM decides when to invoke it. This is the
standard agentic handoff behavior.

Example YAML::

    handoffs:
      triage_agent:
        - agent: billing_agent
          is_deterministic: true
      billing_agent:
        - support_agent            # shorthand for agentic handoff

agent.description: The target agent to hand off to, specified as an AgentModel or agent name string.
agent.title: Agent

is_deterministic.description: When true, the handoff is deterministic: control always transfers to this agent after the source agent completes its turn, without LLM tool-call routing. When false (default), a handoff tool is created and the LLM decides when to invoke it.
is_deterministic.title: Is Deterministic

HandoffRouteModel.title: HandoffRouteModel

HumanInTheLoopModel.description:
Configuration for Human-in-the-Loop tool approval.

This model configures when and how tools require human approval before execution.
It maps to LangChain's HumanInTheLoopMiddleware.

LangChain supports three decision types:
- "approve": Execute tool with original arguments
- "edit": Modify arguments before execution
- "reject": Skip execution with optional feedback message

review_prompt.description: Message shown to the reviewer when approval is requested
review_prompt.title: Review Prompt

allowed_decisions.description: List of allowed decision types for this tool
allowed_decisions.title: Allowed Decisions

HumanInTheLoopModel.title: HumanInTheLoopModel

client_id.title: Client Id

client_secret.description: OAuth client secret for service principal authentication.
client_secret.title: Client Secret

pat.title: Pat

name.title: Name

IndexModel.title: IndexModel

InlineFunctionModel.description:
Inline function model for defining tool code directly in YAML configuration.

This allows you to define simple tools without creating separate Python files.
The code should define a function decorated with @tool from langchain.tools.

SECURITY WARNING: This model uses exec() to execute arbitrary Python code
from the YAML configuration. Only load configurations from trusted sources.
A malicious configuration could execute arbitrary code on the host system.

Example YAML:
    tools:
      calculator:
        name: calculator
        function:
          type: inline
          code: |
            from langchain.tools import tool

            @tool
            def calculator(expression: str) -> str:
                '''Evaluate a mathematical expression.'''
                return str(eval(expression))

The code block must:
- Import @tool from langchain.tools
- Define exactly one function decorated with @tool
- The function name becomes the tool name

human_in_the_loop.description: Human-in-the-loop approval configuration for this tool.

code.description: Python code defining a tool function decorated with @tool
code.title: Code

InlineFunctionModel.title: InlineFunctionModel

InstructedRetrieverModel.description:
Configuration for instructed retrieval with query decomposition and RRF merging.

Groups all schema-aware, LLM-driven features: query decomposition, instruction-aware
reranking, query routing, and result verification. These features share schema context
(columns, constraints) and are co-located here to enforce that dependency at the type
level.

Column metadata is the single source of truth for schema context. Each pipeline
component (decomposition, routing, verification, reranking) generates the specific
context it needs from the structured ``columns`` data:
- Decomposition embeds column info into the JSON schema for ``with_structured_output``
- Routing generates a compact column summary
- Verification generates a context with column descriptions (no operator syntax)
- Reranking uses column names and types for instruction generation

Example:
    ```yaml
    retriever:
      vector_store: *products_vector_store
      instructed:
        columns:
          - name: brand_name
            type: string
            description: "Brand/manufacturer (MILWAUKEE, DEWALT, etc.)"
          - name: price
            type: number
            operators: ["", "<", "<=", ">", ">="]
            description: "Product price in USD"
        constraints:
          - "Prefer recent products"
        decomposition:
          model: *fast_llm
          max_subqueries: 3
          examples:
            - query: "cheap drills"
              filters: {"price <": 100}
        rerank:
          model: *fast_llm
          instructions: "Prioritize by brand preferences"
          top_n: 10
        router:
          model: *fast_llm
          default_mode: standard
        verifier:
          model: *fast_llm
          on_failure: warn_and_retry
    ```

columns.description: Structured column info used by all pipeline components. Column names, types, operators, and descriptions are embedded into JSON schemas and prompts for each component automatically.
columns.title: Columns

decomposition.description: Query decomposition settings for breaking complex queries into subqueries.

router.description: Optional query router for selecting execution mode (standard vs instructed).

verifier.description: Optional result verification with structured feedback for retry.

InstructedRetrieverModel.title: InstructedRetrieverModel

InstructionAwareRerankModel.description:
LLM-based reranking considering user instructions and constraints.

Use fast models (GPT-3.5, Haiku, Llama 3 8B) to minimize latency (~100ms).
Runs AFTER FlashRank as an additional constraint-aware reranking stage.
Skipped for 'standard' mode when auto_bypass=true in router config.

Example:
    ```yaml
    instructed:
      columns:
        - name: brand_name
          type: string
      rerank:
        model: *fast_llm
        instructions: |
          Prioritize results matching price and brand constraints.
        top_n: 10
    ```

model.description: LLM for instruction reranking (fast model recommended)

instructions.description: Custom reranking instructions for constraint prioritization
instructions.title: Instructions

top_n.description: Number of documents to return after instruction reranking
top_n.title: Top N

InstructionAwareRerankModel.title: InstructionAwareRerankModel

MemoryExtractionModel.description:
Configuration for automatic memory extraction and injection.

Controls how the system automatically extracts memories from
conversations and injects relevant context into prompts.

schemas.description: Schema names for structured extraction (e.g. ['user_profile', 'preference', 'episode']). When None, uses unstructured string memories.
schemas.title: Schemas

instructions.description: Custom extraction instructions guiding what to remember. When None, uses langmem's default instructions.
instructions.title: Instructions

auto_inject.description: Automatically search and inject relevant memories into the system prompt before each model call.
auto_inject.title: Auto Inject

auto_inject_limit.description: Maximum number of memories to inject into the prompt.
auto_inject_limit.title: Auto Inject Limit

background_extraction.description: Extract memories in a background thread after each conversation turn (no latency impact on responses).
background_extraction.title: Background Extraction

extraction_model.description: Separate LLM for memory extraction. Can be a smaller, cheaper model. When None, uses the agent's primary model.

query_model.description: Separate LLM for optimizing memory search queries. When None, embeds the raw user message directly.

MemoryExtractionModel.title: MemoryExtractionModel

MemoryModel.description: Memory configuration combining state checkpointing, long-term memory storage, and automatic extraction.

checkpointer.description: Checkpointer for persisting conversation thread state across turns.

store.description: Long-term memory store for cross-thread knowledge (profiles, preferences, episodes).

extraction.description: Automatic memory extraction and injection settings.

MemoryModel.title: MemoryModel

Configuration for middleware that can be applied to agents.

Middleware is defined at the AppConfig level and can be referenced by name
in agent configurations using YAML anchors for reusability.

MonitoringModel.description:
Configuration for production monitoring of GenAI scorers.

Controls which scorers are registered and at what sampling rates against
production traces stored in Unity Catalog via the MLflow 3 scorer
lifecycle API.

Attributes:
    sample_rate: Sampling rate for built-in scorers. Defaults to 1.0 (100%).
    scorers: Optional list of built-in scorer names to enable. When omitted,
        all built-in scorers are registered (safety, completeness,
        relevance_to_query, tool_call_efficiency).
    guidelines: Optional list of guideline configurations for Guidelines
        scorers used in production monitoring.
    guidelines_sample_rate: Sampling rate for Guidelines scorers, which
        invoke an LLM judge per trace and are more expensive. Defaults
        to 0.5 (50%).

scorers.description: Built-in scorer names, glob patterns, or GuardrailModel references to enable. Built-in options: safety, completeness, relevance_to_query, tool_call_efficiency. Supports glob patterns: '*' (all built-in scorers), 'safe*', etc. GuardrailModel entries are converted to scorers via as_scorer(). Defaults to all built-in scorers when omitted.
scorers.title: Scorers

guidelines.description: Guideline configurations for production monitoring Guidelines scorers.
guidelines.title: Guidelines

MonitoringModel.title: MonitoringModel

PromptOptimizationModel.description:
Configuration for prompt optimization using GEPA.

GEPA (Generative Evolution of Prompts and Agents) is an evolutionary
optimizer that uses reflective mutation to improve prompts based on
evaluation feedback.

Example:
    prompt_optimization:
      name: optimize_my_prompt
      prompt: *my_prompt
      agent: *my_agent
      dataset: *my_training_dataset
      reflection_model: databricks-meta-llama-3-3-70b-instruct
      num_candidates: 50

name.description: Unique name for this optimization run.
name.title: Name

prompt.description: Prompt to optimize. If omitted, uses the agent's prompt.

agent.description: Agent whose prompt is being optimized.

dataset.description: Training dataset with input/expectation pairs for fitness evaluation.

reflection_model.description: LLM used for reflective mutation during GEPA optimization.
reflection_model.title: Reflection Model

num_candidates.description: Number of candidate prompts to evaluate per optimization run.
num_candidates.title: Num Candidates

PromptOptimizationModel.title: PromptOptimizationModel

RerankParametersModel.description:
Configuration for reranking retrieved documents.

Supports two reranking options that can be combined:
1. FlashRank (local cross-encoder) - set `model`
2. Databricks server-side reranking - set `columns`

For LLM instruction-aware reranking, use `instructed.rerank` instead.

Example with FlashRank:
    ```yaml
    rerank:
      model: ms-marco-MiniLM-L-12-v2  # FlashRank model
      top_n: 10
    ```

Example with Databricks columns:
    ```yaml
    rerank:
      columns:
        - product_name
        - brand_name
    ```

Available FlashRank models (see https://github.com/PrithivirajDamodaran/FlashRank):
- "ms-marco-TinyBERT-L-2-v2" (~4MB, fastest)
- "ms-marco-MiniLM-L-12-v2" (~34MB, best cross-encoder)
- "rank-T5-flan" (~110MB, best non cross-encoder)
- "ms-marco-MultiBERT-L-12" (~150MB, multilingual 100+ languages)

model.description: FlashRank model name. If None, FlashRank is not used (use columns for Databricks reranking).
model.title: Model

top_n.description: Number of documents to return after reranking. If None, uses search_parameters.num_results.
top_n.title: Top N

cache_dir.description: Directory to cache downloaded model weights. Supports tilde expansion (e.g., ~/.dao_ai).
cache_dir.title: Cache Dir

RerankParametersModel.title: RerankParametersModel

ResourcesModel.description:
Databricks resource declarations used by agents and tools.

Each resource type is a named dictionary so entries can be referenced
elsewhere in the config via YAML anchors.

llms.description: LLM serving endpoint configurations keyed by name.
llms.title: Llms

vector_stores.description: Vector search index configurations for semantic retrieval.
vector_stores.title: Vector Stores

genie_rooms.description: Databricks Genie space configurations for natural-language SQL.
genie_rooms.title: Genie Rooms

volumes.title: Volumes

warehouses.description: SQL warehouse configurations for query execution.
warehouses.title: Warehouses

databases.title: Databases

connections.description: Unity Catalog connection references for MCP and external data sources.
connections.title: Connections

apps.description: Databricks App references used as MCP endpoints or tool backends.
apps.title: Apps

ResourcesModel.title: ResourcesModel

ResponseFormatModel.description:
Configuration for structured response formats.

The response_schema field accepts either a type or a string:
- Type (Pydantic model, dataclass, etc.): Used directly for structured output
- String: First attempts to load as a fully qualified type name, falls back to JSON schema string

This unified approach simplifies the API while maintaining flexibility.

use_tool.description: Strategy for structured output: None (default) = auto-detect from model capabilities, False = force ProviderStrategy (native), True = force ToolStrategy (function calling)
use_tool.title: Use Tool

response_schema.description: Type or string for response format. String attempts FQN import, falls back to JSON schema.
response_schema.title: Response Schema

ResponseFormatModel.title: ResponseFormatModel

RetrieverModel.description: Retriever combining a vector store with search parameters, reranking, and instructed retrieval.

vector_store.description: Vector search index configuration used for similarity search.

columns.description: Columns to return from search results. Defaults to the vector store's columns.
columns.title: Columns

search_parameters.description: Search tuning: number of results, query type, and metadata filters.

rerank.description: Optional reranking configuration. Set to true for defaults, or provide RerankParametersModel for custom settings.
rerank.title: Rerank

instructed.description: Optional instructed retrieval with query decomposition, instruction-aware reranking, routing, and verification.

RetrieverModel.title: RetrieverModel

RouterModel.description:
Select internal execution mode based on query characteristics.

Use fast models (GPT-3.5, Haiku, Llama 3 8B) to minimize latency (~50-100ms).
Routes to internal modes within the same retriever, not external retrievers.
Cross-index routing belongs at the agent/tool-selection level.

Execution Modes:
- "standard": Single similarity_search() for simple keyword/product searches
- "instructed": Decompose -> Parallel Search -> RRF for constrained queries

Example:
    ```yaml
    retriever:
      instructed:
        columns:
          - name: brand_name
            type: string
        router:
          model: *fast_llm
          default_mode: standard
          auto_bypass: true
    ```

model.description: LLM for routing decision (fast model recommended)

auto_bypass.description: Skip Instruction Reranker and Verifier for standard mode
auto_bypass.title: Auto Bypass

RouterModel.title: RouterModel

schema_name.title: Schema Name

permissions.title: Permissions

name.title: Name

VectorSearchEndpoint.title: VectorSearchEndpoint

VectorStoreModel.description:
Configuration model for a Databricks Vector Search store.

Supports two modes:
1. **Use Existing Index**: Provide only `index` (fully qualified name).
   Used for querying an existing vector search index at runtime.
2. **Provisioning Mode**: Provide `source_table` + `embedding_source_column`.
   Used for creating a new vector search index.

Examples:
    Minimal configuration (use existing index):
    ```yaml
    vector_stores:
      products_search:
        index:
          name: catalog.schema.my_index
    ```

    Full provisioning configuration:
    ```yaml
    vector_stores:
      products_search:
        source_table:
          schema: *my_schema
          name: products
        embedding_source_column: description
        endpoint:
          name: my_endpoint
    ```

on_behalf_of_user.description: Use the calling user's identity (OBO). Works in Model Serving and Databricks Apps.
on_behalf_of_user.title: On Behalf Of User

service_principal.description: Service principal for OAuth M2M authentication. Expands to client_id and client_secret.

client_secret.description: OAuth client secret for service principal authentication.
client_secret.title: Client Secret

pat.description: Personal access token for PAT-based authentication.
pat.title: Pat

index.description: Vector search index to query. Required for runtime; auto-generated in provisioning mode.

source_table.description: Source table for provisioning a new vector search index. Omit when using an existing index.

embedding_source_column.description: Column in the source table containing text to embed. Required in provisioning mode.
embedding_source_column.title: Embedding Source Column

embedding_model.description: Embedding model endpoint. Defaults to 'databricks-gte-large-en' in provisioning mode.

endpoint.description: Vector search endpoint hosting the index. Auto-detected in provisioning mode.

source_path.description: Volume path for source data files (alternative to source_table).

checkpoint_path.description: Volume path for sync checkpoint storage.

primary_key.description: Primary key column in the source table. Auto-detected if omitted.
primary_key.title: Primary Key

columns.description: Columns to include in search results.
columns.title: Columns

doc_uri.description: Column name containing document URIs for provenance tracking.
doc_uri.title: Doc Uri

VerifierModel.description:
Validate results against user constraints with structured feedback.

Use fast models (GPT-3.5, Haiku, Llama 3 8B) to minimize latency (~50-100ms).
Skipped for 'standard' mode when auto_bypass=true in router config.
Returns structured feedback for intelligent retry, not blind retry.

Example:
    ```yaml
    retriever:
      instructed:
        columns:
          - name: brand_name
            type: string
        verifier:
          model: *fast_llm
          on_failure: warn_and_retry
          max_retries: 1
    ```

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts before returning with warning
max_retries.title: Max Retries

VerifierModel.title: VerifierModel

description:
Top-level configuration for a DAO AI application.

Defines all resources, agents, tools, and deployment settings
needed to build and deploy an AI agent on Databricks.

version.description: Configuration schema version for forward compatibility.
version.title: Version

variables.description: Named variables (env vars, secrets, literals, composites) reusable via YAML anchors.
variables.title: Variables

service_principals.description: Named service principals for OAuth M2M authentication with Databricks resources.
service_principals.title: Service Principals

schemas.description: Unity Catalog schema references (catalog + schema) used by tables, functions, and prompts.
schemas.title: Schemas

resources.description: Databricks resource declarations: LLMs, vector stores, Genie rooms, tables, warehouses, databases, and more.

retrievers.description: Named retriever configurations combining a vector store with search parameters and optional reranking.
retrievers.title: Retrievers

tools.description: Named tool definitions (Python, factory, inline, Unity Catalog, or MCP) available to agents.
tools.title: Tools

guardrails.description: Named guardrail configurations for evaluating agent responses against quality or safety criteria.
guardrails.title: Guardrails

middleware.description: Named middleware definitions that can be applied to agents for cross-cutting concerns.
middleware.title: Middleware

memory.description: Global memory configuration (checkpointer, store, extraction) shared across agents.

prompts.description: Named prompt definitions backed by the MLflow Prompt Registry.
prompts.title: Prompts

agents.description: Named agent definitions combining an LLM model with tools, guardrails, and middleware.
agents.title: Agents

app.description: Application-level settings: deployment target, model registration, permissions, and orchestration.

evaluation.description: Offline evaluation configuration using MLflow GenAI scorers and a judge model.

optimizations.description: Prompt and cache threshold optimization configurations.

datasets.description: Dataset definitions for provisioning tables with DDL and seed data.
datasets.title: Datasets

unity_catalog_functions.description: Unity Catalog SQL function definitions to create during provisioning.
unity_catalog_functions.title: Unity Catalog Functions

providers.description: Custom provider overrides for dependency injection (advanced usage).
providers.title: Providers

title: AppConfig
